12 Stones Global · Kilo Aupuni · govOS · public service

Title 5 — Business Licenses & Regulations Plain-Language Service Free · no account

A plain-language navigator to Maui County Code Title 5 (Business Licenses & Regulations): how to get a business license, how liquor licensing works (a separate Department of Liquor Control track), the short-term-rental cross-references you can't skip, and who pays. General business licenses go through MAPPS; liquor through Liquor Control — this service explains the law and hands you off to the official systems. Sections marked expanding are being filled in from the ingestion corpus; nothing here is legal advice.

★ Parcel lookup live data

Running a business from a property? Your zoning district decides whether the use is even allowed there before any license matters — a home business (Title 19, ch. 19.67) and a short-term rental (ch. 19.65) each have zoning rules on top of the Title 5 license. Enter a Maui County TMK to see the parcel's zoning and designations, live from the Hawaiʻi Statewide GIS.

Tip: type the digits of your TMK (zone-section-plat-parcel). Don't know it? Open the State Land Use Viewer ↗ to find it on a map.

Live designations: Hawaiʻi Statewide GIS. Business licenses via Maui County MAPPS. No parcel data is stored or fabricated — fetched live. use-allowed overlay for home business / STR

1 · Get a business license sourced

General County business licenses go through MAPPS (which replaced the legacy KivaNet system).

  1. Confirm the use is allowed
    Check your zoning first (Title 19) — a license doesn't override zoning. Home businesses (ch. 19.67) and short-term rentals (ch. 19.65) have separate zoning requirements.
    Expense: free to check · paid by: you (time)
  2. Create a MAPPS account & apply
    Register for MAPPS Customer Self-Service, open the Business Licenses module, complete the steps and upload the required documents.
    Expense: license fee · paid by: applicant
  3. Review & issuance
    The County reviews the application against Title 5 requirements and issues the license; some regulated activities route to additional review.
    Expense: County review · paid by: County (+ applicant fee)
  4. Renew & comply
    Keep the license current and comply with the conditions for your activity (and any state license, e.g. GET, separately).
    Expense: renewal · paid by: licensee
⚖️ Position — James Langford (analysis, not law; a stance beside the rule)A business license should be a low-friction registration that lets a resident earn a living, not a revenue barrier. Where the County requires a license, the process should be fast, online, and cheap — the burden of compliance shouldn't fall hardest on the smallest operator.

County of Maui For Business + MAPPS — Business Licenses (replaced KivaNet). MCC Title 5. per-license-type checklists & fees

2 · Which license or regulation? sourced

Title 5 covers general business licensing and several regulated activities; liquor is a separate department.

ActivityTrackWhere
General business licenseTitle 5 — Business LicensesMAPPS ↗
Sell / serve alcoholLiquor license — Dept. of Liquor ControlLiquor Control ↗
Short-term / vacation rentalTitle 19 zoning (ch. 19.65) + Title 3 tax classTitle 19 ↗
Peddler / solicitor / regulated tradesTitle 5 — see chapterTitle 5 ↗

MCC Title 5 (§§5.06–5.26) + County Liquor Control. full chapter list (peddlers, specific regulated trades)

3 · Liquor license classes sourced

Liquor is regulated by the Department of Liquor Control (its own rules, MC-08), not the general business-license track. Classes include:

ClassUse
ManufacturerMake alcoholic beverages
Wholesale dealerSell to retailers
Retail dealerSell packaged for off-premises
DispenserSell/serve for on-premises consumption
RestaurantServe with meals
ClubMembers' club service
SpecialEvents / limited-term

County of Maui Department of Liquor Control + Liquor Control Commission; rules Title MC-08. Apply via the DLC (separate from MAPPS). verbatim MC-08 class definitions & fees

4 · Short-term rentals — the cross-reference you can't skip sourced

An STR is the classic trap: a business license alone is not enough.

Operating a short-term / vacation rental on Maui touches three titles at once: Title 19 zoning decides whether it's even allowed and on what permit (short-term rental homes, ch. 19.65; B&Bs ch. 19.64), Title 3 places it in the TVR-STRH tax class (a higher rate), and Title 5 business licensing applies to the operation. Clear all three before advertising — see the Title 19 and Title 3 services.

MCC Title 5 (STR/TVR licensing cross-refs, per the MCC inventory) + Title 19 ch. 19.65 + Title 3 classification (TVR-STRH). STR registry + enforcement coordination

5 · Enforcement expanding

How an unlicensed or non-compliant business is addressed. Not legal advice.

Title 5 carries its own licensing requirements and remedies, backed by the County's general penalty provisions (Title 1) for code violations; liquor violations are enforced separately by the Department of Liquor Control (license suspension/revocation + fines under its rules). Note: the Title 19 enforcement chapter (§19.530.030) reaches MCC titles 8, 12, 14, 16, 18, 19 and 20 — it does not list Title 5, so business-license enforcement runs on Title 5 / Title 1 / Liquor-Control tracks, not the zoning-enforcement engine.

MCC Title 5 + Title 1 (general penalty) + Liquor Control. Specific Title 5 penalty sections expanding pending verbatim ingest.

6 · Who pays — the charter lens analysis

Labeled analysis — licensing read through two charters. Not adopted law; an interpretive lens beside the Code.

Maui County Charter — lensUnder the Maui County Charter, the County licenses and regulates businesses through Finance/departments, with the Department of Liquor Control a dedicated regulator; license fees are set by ordinance / rule. The Charter frames licensing as a regulatory and revenue function delegated to the administration.
Sovereign Charter (12 Stones) — lensUnder the Sovereign Charter (12 Stones) lens, the right to earn an honest living is foundational; a license is the County's permission to exercise a pre-existing right. The lens reads licensing as something that should be minimal, fast, and cheap for residents — with regulation focused where there is real public risk (alcohol, STR housing impact), not used as a general toll on small enterprise.
⚖️ Position — analysis, not lawRegulate where there's genuine public risk — alcohol, short-term-rental pressure on housing — and make everything else a two-minute online registration. The fee should recover processing cost, not gate the resident out of their own livelihood. Same license, read two ways.

7 · Rules → Code: candidates for codification analysis

⚖️ Analysis / position — not lawProvisions that, in our assessment, should be codified / streamlined in Title 5 so the binding standard and cost are readable from the Code. Citations are verbatim-checked; the recommendation is a stance beside the rule.
  • Single published fee schedule. License fees are spread across rules and practice. → Codify a one-page Title 5 fee schedule so any operator can read their cost up front. expanding
  • STR cross-title coordination. An STR clears Title 19 + Title 3 + Title 5 separately, with no single front door. → Codify a unified STR registration that confirms zoning, tax class, and license together.
  • Small-operator on-ramp. The smallest businesses bear the same process as the largest. → Codify a simplified track (low-risk activities) so licensing doesn't deter resident enterprise.

Analysis grounded in MCC Title 5 + the County business & liquor licensing framework; contents expanding pending verbatim ingest.

Free now (no account): this page, the live parcel lookup, the licensing navigator, and the license/liquor-class tables are all Tier 1 — free. Coming — Tier 3 (verified tools): a license-readiness checklist, an STR cross-title checker, and an AI licensing assistant will be verification-gated (identity-verified). Identity is verified through the payment processor — only verified-status is stored here, never the raw ID. No live billing is enabled (Stripe keys are environment references only); the actual license application happens on MAPPS or with Liquor Control.
govOS · Kilo Aupuni · 12 Stones Global · free public service · sourced to Maui County Code Title 5 (Business Licenses & Regulations) via Municode + County of Maui For Business / MAPPS + Department of Liquor Control (Title MC-08) + Hawaiʻi Statewide GIS · first version 2026-06-18, expanding · Built by the County Service Module Engine (config + sourced content — no bespoke page) · STR cross-refs to Title 19 (ch. 19.64/19.65) + Title 3 (TVR-STRH class) · not legal advice · public records only · hands off to MAPPS / Liquor Control for the official record.
